title: symphyotrichum lanceolatum var. hirsuticaule
intro: Symphyotrichum lanceolatum var. hirsuticaule, commonly known as hairy panicled aster, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the daisy family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids in its leaves and flowers. The hairy panicled aster is said to offer several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal and therapeutic properties.
